column5: Bug Description
column6: Fix Applied

column5: Double HTTP request on admin login — Login.tsx made a direct axios.post AND called login() which made a second identical request; first token was silently discarded
column6: Admin login now delegates entirely to AuthContext.login(); no direct axios.post in Login.tsx

column5: Token/user destructured from wrong response shape — admin endpoint returns { token user } but code destructured { token driver } so user was always undefined
column6: Fixed destructure to use correct shape per role

column5: Dead unreachable else branch — inner if(role==='driver') was always true making '/admin-home' redirect dead code
column6: Removed dead code with restructured logic

column5: No form validation or inline error display — errors shown via alert() only
column6: Added loginError state with inline form error display and blank-field guard

column5: useEffect for axios header sync had no dependency array — ran on every render
column6: Added [user] as dependency array

column5: logout() called navigate('/') causing double-navigation race with Logout.tsx page
column6: Removed navigate('/') from logout(); Logout page handles navigation

column5: exitOrg() stored literal string 'null' as token when superadmin_token key was missing — corrupted auth state
column6: Added null guard; navigates to /select-org if superadmin_token absent

column5: switchOrg() could store null as superadmin_token with no active session
column6: Added explicit null guard that throws error if no token present

column5: isInsideOrg and activeOrgName were non-reactive localStorage reads — Navbar org banner never updated after switchOrg/exitOrg
column6: Converted both to useState updated in switchOrg / exitOrg / logout

column5: login() swallowed errors with alert() preventing Login.tsx from displaying inline errors
column6: Changed catch block to re-throw error

column5: changePassword never verified old password — any authenticated user could change any password freely
column6: Added bcrypt.compare(oldPassword) check; returns 400 if mismatch

column5: changePassword and getUserProfile manually re-verified JWT instead of using req.user from protect middleware — crashes on cookie-based auth
column6: Replaced manual JWT extraction with User.findById(req.user.id)

column5: changePassword called bcrypt.hash() before save AND model pre-save hook also hashes — double-hash made new password permanently unverifiable
column6: Assign plain newPassword directly; let model hook handle hashing

column5: No minimum password length validation server-side
column6: Added check rejecting passwords shorter than 8 characters with 400 response

column5: company_admin and dispatcher blocked from change-password endpoint (only admin and driver allowed) — 403 Forbidden
column6: Updated authorizeRoles to include company_admin and dispatcher

column5: Successful password change redirected to '/' (landing page) instead of the user's dashboard
column6: Redirects to /dashboard for drivers; /admin-home for admins/company_admins/dispatchers

column5: No client-side minimum password length validation
column6: Added newPassword.trim().length < 8 check before submission

column5: useEffect auto-filled Current Password field with driver's plain-text stored password — serious security violation
column6: useEffect now only clears all three password fields on driver selection change; no auto-fill

column5: login() swallowed errors with alert() instead of re-throwing to caller
column6: Changed to throw error so Login.tsx handles error display

column5: Field name mismatch broke registration — controller expected companyName/adminName but frontend sends name; every registration failed with 400
column6: Changed to accept name (with companyName as legacy fallback); removed adminName requirement

column5: Plan field from request body never applied to org document — every org silently created with default 'driver' plan regardless of selection
column6: Added plan validation and explicitly set subscription.plan and subscription.status on org at creation

column5: Double password hashing — controller called bcrypt.hash() then model pre-save hook also hashed — login was impossible for all self-registered users
column6: Removed manual bcrypt.hash() call; pre-save hook handles hashing

column5: organizationId missing from registration response user object — AuthContext had no org scope after signup
column6: Added organizationId: organization._id to response user object

column5: company_admin and dispatcher blocked from changing password
column6: Added company_admin and dispatcher to allowed roles

column5: Org context banner and sticky header both had top:0 — header rendered on top of banner hiding it
column6: Made header top dynamic: top: isInsideOrg ? '33px' : '0'

column5: All notification fetch() calls missing Authorization header — unlike axios fetch doesn't pick up axios.defaults — all returning 401
column6: Added Authorization: Bearer <token> to all four fetch() calls in Navbar

column5: Crash if org.subscription is undefined — org.subscription.status accessed without null guard
column6: Added const sub = org.subscription ?? { status: 'trialing' plan: 'bundle' } as defensive fallback

column5: Stripe webhook routes mounted AFTER bodyParser.json() — raw body buffer consumed before express.raw() could see it — every webhook failed signature verification
column6: Moved paymentRoutes and subscriptionRoutes to be mounted BEFORE app.use(bodyParser.json())

column5: Server fallback port was 7000 instead of 8000 — breaking all local dev API connections
column6: Changed fallback to 8000

column5: createCheckout had zero error handling — any Stripe/DB error left the HTTP request hanging

column5: getCurrentSubscription and createBillingPortal had zero error handling
column6: Wrapped both in try/catch returning 500; added organizationId null guards

column5: Expired trial reported as active — if trialEndsAt was past but Stripe webhook missed org treated as trialing (open access to gated features)

column5: Webhook: dead getOrgId helper; no try/catch around switch block; missing STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET guard; checkout.session.completed could write undefined to subscription.plan

column5: No try/catch — DB errors propagated as unhandled rejections

column5: Expired trial bypassed feature gate — trialing with past trialEndsAt still treated as active

column5: No loading state — page was invisible until data loaded with no feedback to user

column5: Trial countdown showed only date not days remaining; no color warning when expiring soon

column5: Plan button showed wrong label — used currentPlan truthiness but org always has default plan; couldn't distinguish trial vs paid

column5: Annual pricing footnote used marginTop: '-40px' overlapping plan cards

column5: make / model / year marked required:true — frontend allows blank causing Mongoose 500 errors on valid submissions
column6: Made fields optional with default: '' for strings; unitNumber remains the only required field

column5: createVehicle missing null guard for organizationId — vehicle created with null org on broken JWT

column5: createVehicle spread req.body before setting organizationId — malicious client could inject a different org's ID

column5: PUT route missing uploadVehiclePhotos middleware — photo uploads on edit silently ignored

column5: Stale token closure — headers built from localStorage once at mount; re-login would use old token in all calls

column5: fetchVehicles not memoized; missing from useEffect dependency array

column5: No safety check that API response is array — crash if API returns non-array

column5: odometer field used loose falsy check — value of '0' stripped from payload

column5: Notes textarea placed outside formGrid div — gridColumn: '1 / -1' CSS had no effect

column5: Delete button had no loading guard — double-click fired multiple concurrent DELETE requests

column5: Date sort produced NaN when date was null/undefined — sort order broken

column5: updateClaim used $set: { 'claims.$': req.body } — overwrote entire subdocument discarding fields not in req.body

column5: handleSaveTemplate sent form without checking name was non-empty — Mongoose required error surfaced as silent 500

column5: handleSaveSchedule sent without checking vehicleId or templateId

column5: All mutation handlers used fetch without checking response.ok — server errors silently ignored

column5: No required-field validation before save; no stock check before use; all handlers swallowed server errors

column5: vehicleId/title/startDate/expiryDate all required in schema but no frontend validation — unhandled 500s; no date ordering check; handleAddClaim missing required checks

column5: create stored passwords as plaintext — driverLogin uses bcrypt.compare() so manually created drivers could never log in
